hero.description: Four on-chain primitives. Three composable flows. Everything you need to build agent-to-agent payments on Ergo — from a single API call to a full community currency.

reserve.title: Reserve
reserve.subtitle: Capital backing
reserve.description: An on-chain contract that holds ERG or tokens as collateral. Anyone can verify the reserve ratio at any time. The reserve is the bank — no middleman.

note.title: Note
note.subtitle: Programmable IOU
note.description: A bearer token representing a claim against a reserve. Notes can be transferred, split, and redeemed. The acceptance predicate defines who can receive or redeem the note.

tracker.title: Tracker
tracker.subtitle: On-chain accounting
tracker.description: Tracks cumulative balances, credit usage, and redemption history across all notes issued from a reserve. Think of it as a public ledger that anyone can verify.

predicate.title: Acceptance Predicate
predicate.subtitle: Programmable trust rule
predicate.description: The condition under which an agent will accept a note as payment. Conditions can include task hash verification, deadline checks, credential proofs, or any on-chain data.

apiCall.title: Flow A: Agent buys API call
apiCall.description: One call. One proof. No persistent account.

steps.label: Agent creates note
steps.detail: 0.001 ERG face value, provider address in R4

steps.label: Provider checks predicate
steps.detail: Verifies note value, reserve backing, deadline

steps.label: Provider delivers response
steps.detail: Returns API result, marks note as spent

steps.label: Settlement
steps.detail: Note burned, ERG released from reserve to provider

credit.title: Flow B: Agent pays on credit
credit.description: Reserve deployed. Notes issued. Tracker monitors.

steps.label: Deploy reserve
steps.detail: Lock ERG in reserve contract with credit limit

steps.label: Issue notes
steps.detail: Create notes against reserve up to credit limit

steps.label: Agent spends notes
steps.detail: Notes transferred to providers, tracker updated

steps.label: Auto-settlement
steps.detail: When threshold hit, reserve settles outstanding notes

community.title: Flow C: Community reserve + tracker
community.description: A local marketplace, a compute co-op, an agent network.

steps.label: Issue community notes
steps.detail: Members receive notes proportional to contribution

steps.label: Local commerce
steps.detail: Notes accepted within community, predicates define rules

steps.label: Redemption
steps.detail: Members redeem notes for ERG from reserve anytime

fleetSdk.desc: Build transactions in your browser or Node.js. Best starting point for agent payments.

sigmaRust.desc: High-performance signing and serialization. Ideal for production agent infrastructure.

appKit.desc: Full-featured SDK for backend services, bots, and server-side agents.

ergoNodeApi.desc: Direct access to the node. Submit transactions, query UTXOs, watch addresses.

status.heading: Stack status
status.description: Five layers are live. Two are open problems. Join the conversation on GitHub — these are the most interesting unsolved problems in agent money.

babelFees.title: Babel Fees
babelFees.desc: Pay fees in any token — key for agents

cta.title: Building agents? Talk to us.
cta.description: Book a design session, get code review, or join the builders list. We'll respond within 24 hours.

quickstartPage.heroDescription: No theory. No setup ceremony. One npm package, 30 lines, one command — and you see a real transaction on Ergo testnet explorer.
quickstartPage.prereq2: A testnet address (create one in Nautilus wallet → Settings → Testnet mode)
quickstartPage.prereq3: Testnet ERG from faucet at testnet.ergofaucet.org
quickstartPage.step1Note: Fleet SDK is the official TypeScript/JS SDK for Ergo. Works in Node.js and browsers.
quickstartPage.step2Note: This script fetches your testnet UTxOs, builds a transaction, and outputs the unsigned TX object.
quickstartPage.step3Note: You'll see the unsigned transaction JSON. Sign it with Nautilus (browser) or a server-side key, then POST to /api/v1/transactions.
quickstartPage.step4Note: This extends the basic TX to create a Note — a bearer instrument with a task hash register. The receiver redeems it against a Reserve.
quickstartPage.explorerDescription: After submitting, your transaction appears on Ergo testnet explorer. Paste your TX ID at:
quickstartPage.nextCard1Desc: Reserve, Note, Tracker, Acceptance Predicate — the full technical reference
quickstartPage.nextCard2Desc: From environment setup to mainnet deployment — a full builder guide
quickstartPage.nextCard3Desc: See working agent payment flows on Ergo testnet, open source
quickstartPage.nextCard4Desc: Open source examples: basic payment, Note IOU, acceptance predicate
quickstartPage.ctaDescription: The quickstart gets you moving. The full architecture reference and 7-step playbook are waiting.
